methods.description: Process for analyzing major elements in juvenile fragments using scanning electron microscopy and energy-dispersive spectrometry to determine the composition and origin of tephra deposits.

steps.description: Juvenile fragments (pumice, scoria and glass shards) of samples selected from each deposit were mounted on epoxy resin and suitably polished for microprobe analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: mount and polish juvenile fragments for microprobe analysis

steps.description: Microprobe analyses performed on a JEOL JSM 5310 scanning electron microscope (15 kV, ZAF Correction Routine) equipped with an energy-dispersive spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: perform microprobe analysis using SEM and EDS

steps.description: Correction for matrix effects was performed using INCA version 4.08 software that used the XPP correction routine, based on a Phi-Ro-Zeta approach.
steps.searchable_summary: apply matrix effect correction using INCA software

steps.description: Primary calibration was performed using international mineral and glass standards.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate using international mineral and glass standards

steps.description: Precision and accuracy were assessed using the rhyolitic Lipari obsidian ID3506 and basaltic Laki 1783 AD tephra as secondary standards.
steps.searchable_summary: assess precision and accuracy using secondary standards

steps.description: Correlation of the analyzed tephra with proximal deposits or equivalents from different sites was based on a comparison of the new data with published SEM-EDS and WDS data where available.
steps.searchable_summary: compare new data with published SEM-EDS and WDS data

methods.description: Procedure for sampling visible and cryptotephras, preparing samples for analysis, and selecting materials for major element characterization.

steps.description: Two visible tephras and four cryptotephras were sampled and analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: sample visible and cryptotephras for analysis

steps.description: Sediment samples were disaggregated in distilled water and wet sieved at 63, 90, 125 and 250 μm to remove the finegrained fraction.
The sieved material was cleaned with an ultrasonic probe and dried at 60°C.
A magnetic separator was used for scoria-rich samples.
steps.searchable_summary: separate scoria-rich samples using magnetic separator

steps.description: Lithology was described using an optical microscope and fresh glass fragments were picked from selected samples for major element characterization.
steps.searchable_summary: describe lithology and select glass fragments for analysis
